packs.title: Digital Product Core Ad Prompts
packs.summary: Twelve screen-first image-ad routes for apps, SaaS tools, AI products, developer products, collaboration tools, marketplaces, commerce products, and other software products where the UI is the product proof.
packs.whenToUse: Use when the product is a digital interface and the ad needs to keep the app screen large, inspectable, product-specific, and central to the composition.
requiredInputs: approved UI reference or conceptual UI caveat
exact exterior headline and CTA, or NONE
riskGates: real customer names or private data
exterior copy that was not supplied

families.title: Product Proof Crop
families.summary: A tight product proof ad where the approved UI occupies most of the frame and the surrounding scene stays minimal.
families.businessGoal: prove the digital product visually before adding messaging, claims, or lifestyle context
families.scene: a large, central, inspectable app or browser surface with restrained environment, clean edge detail, and controlled negative space
families.focus: the exact product UI surface and the interface mechanic that makes the product recognizable
families.decision: whether a direct UI proof crop should lead the campaign
elements: small product-name lockup only if supplied

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied exterior headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: No body copy, metrics, reviews, badges, or benefit bullets unless supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a premium screen-first product proof ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The screen should occupy roughly 70-85% of the composition and remain large, central, inspectable, and product-specific. Show the approved core UI surface described in the product facts clearly in the approved device policy. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] only as atmosphere. Do not redesign the UI, invent fake data, add claims, metrics, reviews, partner logos, app-store badges, or exterior body copy. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: UI must stay large and inspectable
do not create a generic dashboard
do not add exterior copy unless supplied
families.avoid: generic phone/laptop ad, tiny UI, fake analytics, fake product data, unsupported claims, app-store badges, partner logos

families.title: Single Device App Packshot
families.renderingMode: premium device or app-window still life
families.summary: A clean packshot where one device or app window becomes the product object.
families.businessGoal: make the software feel like a tangible product without drifting into a generic hardware ad
families.scene: one crisp app window, browser window, laptop, monitor, or phone on a refined stage with restrained lighting
families.focus: clear product silhouette, recognizable UI, and controlled product-world atmosphere
families.decision: whether a single-object product packshot is the strongest base asset
elements: single approved device or app window

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Allowed readable text is limited to product-identifying UI labels from the brief.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a single-device product packshot for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. Treat the device or app window as the product object, with the UI crisp and central. Use one clean approved device policy from the product facts on a refined product stage. Keep the silhouette simple, the screen large, and the background restrained. Allowed readable text must come only from the product facts. No badges, ratings, reviews, claims, benefit bullets, or fake proof. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not imply unsupported platform availability
families.avoid: wrong device policy, stock hardware ad, fake store badge, tiny product screen, generic dashboard

families.title: Multi-Surface Product Wall
families.summary: A launch-energy wall with one dominant hero UI and supporting surfaces that repeat, crop, or skeletonize approved states.
families.businessGoal: show product depth without inventing unsupported screens or fake proof
families.scene: one dominant product screen with two or three secondary surfaces arranged in depth against a precise product-world backdrop
families.focus: product breadth, interface continuity, and a clear hero surface
families.decision: whether a multi-surface system tells the product story better than a single crop

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Secondary surfaces must be cropped, repeated, skeletonized, or supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a multi-surface product wall for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. Use one dominant hero screen and two or three supporting surfaces that repeat, crop, or skeletonize the same approved product UI from the product facts. Do not invent unsupported secondary product states, fake user content, metrics, claims, or new product data. Build launch energy through depth, device layering, and [BRAND_PALETTE], while keeping the main UI inspectable. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: supporting screens cannot introduce fake data
families.avoid: unsupported secondary screens, fake dashboards, unreadable product wall, excessive glow, generic SaaS montage

families.title: Product In Context Moment
families.summary: A believable work or everyday setting where the product screen remains the hero and the environment only explains the use moment.
families.businessGoal: connect the digital product to a real workflow without implying endorsement or inventing private data
families.scene: a plausible workspace, studio, review session, collaboration moment, or public work context with the product UI sharp and central
families.focus: use moment clarity, audience recognition, and product-screen dominance
families.decision: whether the campaign needs human workflow context before abstract product routes
elements: anonymous hands or silhouette only if useful

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: No readable notes, private documents, customer names, or metrics.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a realistic in-context product ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The environment supports the use moment but cannot overtake the product. Show the approved core UI surface from the product facts clearly in a believable setting for the target audience and use moment. Props should be secondary and non-readable. Human presence, if any, should be anonymous and not imply endorsement. No readable private data, notes, signs, logos, names, addresses, claims, or metrics. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: background text must be absent or blurred
no real people or customer identifiers
families.avoid: lifestyle scene where product is a prop, readable private data, customer names, fake proof, generic workplace stock look

families.title: Feature Mechanic Spotlight
families.renderingMode: screen composite with restrained highlight treatment
families.summary: A focused feature ad that highlights the product's primary mechanic without covering or replacing the UI.
families.businessGoal: make the core mechanic legible without using fake tutorials, popovers, or claims
families.scene: one product screen with a restrained glow, lens, ring, ribbon, cursor path, or focus treatment around the primary mechanic
families.focus: the real UI mechanic described in the brief
families.decision: whether a feature-mechanic route can explain the product without extra copy

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Feature labels must come from allowed readable text in the product facts.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a feature spotlight for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE] focused on the primary product mechanic described in the product facts. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The highlight must guide attention to the real product mechanic without covering or replacing the UI. Use one restrained glow, ring, ribbon, lens, cursor path, or focus treatment around that mechanic. Do not add fake popovers, fake results, explanatory body copy, metrics, tutorials, claims, or new UI states. If a label appears, it must come from the allowed readable text in the product facts. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: no explanatory body copy unless supplied
families.avoid: overdone glow, fake tutorial step, invented feature label, hidden UI, unsupported AI claim

families.title: Digital Product Launch Hero
families.renderingMode: premium launch poster with product UI proof
families.summary: A vertical launch-style ad where atmosphere can be expressive but the product UI remains the proof object.
families.businessGoal: create launch energy while avoiding abstract-only posters and unsupported claims
families.scene: one large product UI surface with premium lighting, depth, and safe negative space around it
families.focus: product identity, launch atmosphere, and screen-first proof
families.decision: whether a launch hero should be more atmospheric while still anchored in product UI
elements: small product identity only if supplied

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Do not add slogans, launch dates, proof claims, partner marks, or app-store badges unless supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a vertical launch hero for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. Brand atmosphere can be expressive, but the product screen remains the proof object. Use [BRAND_PALETTE] for premium lighting, depth, and negative space around one large product UI surface. Do not create an abstract-only poster. Do not add launch dates, partner marks, testimonials, app-store badges, slogans, claims, or body copy unless exact copy is supplied. Avoid [AVOID].
families.avoid: abstract poster with no product, fake launch date, unsupported slogan, generic AI glow, app-store badge

families.title: Kinetic Product Motion Still
families.summary: A static ad that implies product motion around a crisp UI surface.
families.businessGoal: suggest workflow momentum while preserving inspectable UI and avoiding fake progress results
families.scene: one or more product surfaces with motion trails, cursor paths, workflow paths, card depth, or collaboration glow around the UI
families.focus: movement around the product mechanic without blurring or replacing the screen
families.decision: whether motion language should carry the campaign into animated variants

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Do not add progress percentages, counters, or results.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a kinetic static ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. Motion energy belongs around the product screen; the UI itself must stay crisp and inspectable. Suggest the primary product mechanic described in the product facts through motion trails, swipe depth, route light, scroll freeze, audio wave, cursor path, workflow path, or collaboration glow. Do not blur the UI. Do not add fake counters, progress percentages, results, or claims. Avoid [AVOID].
families.avoid: blurred UI, fake progress percentage, sci-fi clutter, generic motion streaks, unreadable screen

families.title: Creative UI Collage
families.summary: A premium collage with a dominant product screen and abstract supporting tiles that never introduce unsupported data.
families.businessGoal: show a broader product world while keeping the approved UI central and truthful
families.scene: central product screen surrounded by abstract cards, content fragments, panels, or skeletonized surfaces related to the product category
families.focus: product world, visual richness, and safe abstraction
families.decision: whether an editorial collage can make the product feel richer without adding fake proof

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Supporting tiles must be non-readable, skeletonized, or approved.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a premium creative UI collage for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The central product screen must stay dominant and recognizable. Surround the main UI with abstract tiles, cards, panels, or content fragments related to the product category described in the product facts. All supporting tiles must be non-readable, skeletonized, or abstract unless approved screenshots are supplied. No real names, handles, addresses, ratings, reviews, metrics, partner logos, or unsupported claims. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: supporting cards cannot contain fake data
families.avoid: generic mood collage, real names, fake social handles, metrics, partner logos, unreadable central screen

families.title: Core Mechanic Hero
families.summary: A product-specific hero that dramatizes the one mechanic that makes the software unmistakable.
families.businessGoal: make the product's operating model visible without relying on claims or body copy
families.scene: a dominant product UI with visual atmosphere built from the primary mechanic: prompts, workflows, diffs, cards, route lines, threads, or command bars
families.focus: the primary mechanic as the recognizable product signature
families.decision: whether the core mechanic should carry the campaign identity

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Mechanic cues should be visual or allowed UI labels, not proof claims.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a product-specific mechanic hero for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The visual system should dramatize the one mechanic that makes this product unmistakable. Build the scene around the primary product mechanic described in the product facts, such as route lines, pins, audio rows, threads, huddles, workflows, cards, canvases, checkout paths, command bars, prompts, saves, or shares. Use the mechanic as atmosphere, not as proof text. No fake metrics, outputs, private data, or claims. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: mechanic must come from product facts
families.avoid: generic AI sparkle, fake metrics, unsupported product outputs, abstract-only scene, unreadable UI

families.title: Screen Detail Macro
families.summary: A close crop into the interface that still keeps the product recognizable.
families.businessGoal: turn the product's tactile UI details into a premium ad without inventing labels or data
families.scene: a close crop into the approved core UI surface or mechanic with glass edge, subtle reflection, and shallow depth
families.focus: screen texture, product detail, and the specific UI mechanic
families.decision: whether a macro detail can create premium product desire

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Do not add explanatory labels unless supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a macro screen-detail ad for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. Even in a close crop, the product must remain recognizable. Crop into the approved core UI surface or primary mechanic described in the product facts with tactile glass, screen edge, subtle reflection, and shallow depth. Keep the focal UI crisp. Do not invent search results, messages, addresses, ratings, names, metrics, or explanatory labels. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: do not crop into a generic texture
families.avoid: unrecognizable macro, fake search results, fake messages, fake metrics, excessive reflection

families.title: Product In Public Moment
families.summary: A public or everyday setting with the product screen sharp, large, and still dominant.
families.businessGoal: broaden the product's use moment without leaking data, implying endorsement, or hiding the UI
families.scene: a plausible public, work, travel, creator, shopping, or collaboration moment with atmospheric background and a large sharp product screen
families.focus: everyday relevance while preserving product proof
families.decision: whether the campaign should place the product in the broader world
elements: anonymous user presence only if useful

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Background text, signage, notifications, and documents must be absent or blurred.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a public or everyday product moment for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The product screen should be sharp and large while the world around it stays atmospheric. Show the product in a plausible public, work, travel, creator, shopping, or collaboration moment that fits the audience and use moment in the product facts. Background text, signage, brands, notifications, and documents must be absent or blurred. No endorsement cues, private data, location claims, or fake results. Avoid [AVOID].
constraints: screen must be sharp and large
background text must be absent or blurred
families.avoid: product as tiny prop, readable signage, brand logos, private documents, fake results

families.title: Gallery Swipe System
families.summary: A vertical stack of app cards or screens that implies product depth while keeping the front surface dominant.
families.businessGoal: make the product feel explorable across a campaign system without inventing secondary data
families.scene: front product surface with stacked cards, screens, or app surfaces receding behind it
families.focus: product depth, swipe rhythm, and a dominant readable front surface
families.decision: whether a stackable gallery system should become the social asset language

copyRules.headline: Use exact supplied headline only; otherwise omit.
copyRules.supportingText: Secondary surfaces should be cropped, repeated, or skeletonized unless approved screenshots are supplied.
copyRules.cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit.

families.promptTemplate: Create a vertical gallery-swipe product system for [SUBJECT_REFERENCE]. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The front product surface must be readable and dominant. Use a stack of app cards, screens, or surfaces to imply product depth. Secondary surfaces should be cropped, repeated, or skeletonized unless approved screenshots are supplied. Do not invent secondary data, user identities, ratings, metrics, addresses, messages, or partner marks. Avoid [AVOID].
families.avoid: fake secondary screens, user identities, ratings, metrics, addresses, partner marks, unreadable front card
